guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"6nrlq","text":"\"Based on our experience, we believe that more extensive embryological and molecular work with sepsids is very feasible, although some may find the need to provide the colonies with fresh cow dung objectionable.\"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"12mai","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"51qrp","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Locate free-ranging, grass fed, and antibiotic-free Milking Shorthorn cows ( Bos taurus ) We used: Cows in the Tilden Regional Park in Berkeley, CA
Startle the cows by loud shouting Cows should rapidly stand up, defecate, and move away from the source of the annoyance.
Collect dung in ZipLoc bags (1 gallon)
Snap-frozen and store at −80 °C Thaw dung aliquots at 4 °C and moisten slightly before use.
creator: Lenny Teytelman
